title: Two days WFH, weekly 'no vehicle day': Delhi unveils fuel-saving steps after PM's call
content: Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta has unveiled significant austerity measures, slashing official vehicle fuel quotas by 20% and mandating Metro use for ministers and officers every Monday. A weekly 'No Vehicle Day' and a six-month ban on new vehicle purchases are also in effect.

title: US moving to drop fraud case against Gautam Adani, settle SEC suit: Report
content: US authorities are reportedly moving to end fraud cases against Gautam Adani, with the Department of Justice potentially dropping criminal charges and the SEC nearing a settlement in its civil case. This development could offer a significant legal reprieve for the billionaire and the Adani Group, which has been facing regulatory scrutiny.

title: Precision, production & power: How long can US last in a war with China?
content: During a Beijing visit, President Trump observed China's military might, signaling a shift in global power. While the US faces munitions shortages and slow production, China's defense industry operates at wartime pace, excelling in drones, AI, and satellite tech. This evolving warfare, particularly concerning Taiwan, highlights a contest for future global dominance.

title: 'While AI writes 90% of company code, we hired a lot more...': Anthropic CFO Krishna Rao
content: Anthropic's CFO reveals AI now writes 90% of the company's code, yet hiring is at an all-time high. AI acts as a productivity accelerant, automating routine tasks and allowing human employees to focus on oversight, judgment, and strategy.

title: 'Janta mandate gone for a toss': BJP claims new Kerala CM will be controlled by IUML
content: Following the Congress's announcement of VD Satheesan as Kerala's next chief minister, the BJP launched a sharp attack, alleging the decision was influenced by the Indian Union Muslim League. BJP leaders claimed the Congress leadership surrendered to its alliance partner, accusing them of being remote-controlled and bowing to the IUML's demands due to political dependence in the state.

title: Mumbai Indians script unique IPL history as Bumrah captains vs Punjab Kings
content: Jasprit Bumrah captained Mumbai Indians for the first time, marking a unique IPL record. MI became the first team to have three Indian captains in a single season: Hardik Pandya, Suryakumar Yadav, and Bumrah. This leadership shift highlights an unusual season for the franchise, with Bumrah making his captaincy debut after 156 matches.

title: Meta's highest-paid employee is not happy with 'just money motivated' tag for his team
content: Meta SuperIntelligence Lab chief Alexandr Wang is pushing back on the idea that his team joined for the money. On the Core Memory podcast, Wang said the real draw was compute access, talent density, and research freedom—not the $100 million packages Meta reportedly offered. He also addressed the viral soup story involving Mark Zuckerberg and an OpenAI recruit.

title: How VD Satheesan became Kerala CM: Inside Congress' 10-day showdown
content: After a tense 10-day wait and intense internal lobbying, VD Satheesan has been named the next Chief Minister of Kerala. The Congress-led UDF's sweeping victory in the assembly elections triggered a fierce leadership race, with supporters of Satheesan, KC Venugopal, and Ramesh Chennithala campaigning vigorously. The party high command ultimately made the decision after extensive consultations.

title: Delta Airlines 'rejects' Starlink, reportedly wants to 'wait' for Amazon Leo; Elon Musk
content: Elon Musk stated Delta Airlines rejected Starlink's satellite internet, claiming the airline aimed to make the service "painful, difficult and expensive" for customers. Musk emphasized Starlink's mandate for effortless, portal-free WiFi, contrasting it with Delta's approach. The article also explores whether Delta's decision will lead to customer loss.

title: ‘Country has been watching with awe’: Goenka hails Sooryavanshi after India A call-up
content: Lucknow Super Giants owner Sanjiv Goenka lauded teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's inclusion in the India A squad for the Sri Lanka tri-series. The 15-year-old's rapid rise, marked by an U19 World Cup final century, earned praise. Ayush Badoni also features, with selectors focusing on grooming young talent for future international commitments.

title: 'Not recusing': HC judge initiates contempt against Kejriwal; sends case to new bench
content: Delhi high court judge Swarana Kanta Sharma announced contempt proceedings against discharged accused in the excise policy case for posting defamatory material. The judge stated she cannot remain silent against vilifying content targeting her and the court. This action follows Arvind Kejriwal and others boycotting proceedings after the judge declined to recuse herself.

title: UP thunderstorms: Death toll rises to 117; Yogi govt announces Rs 4 lakh compensation
content: Uttar Pradesh reels from devastating rains and thunderstorms, claiming over 100 lives and causing significant damage to homes and livestock. The state government has pledged Rs 4 lakh compensation for each deceased and is actively monitoring relief efforts. Meanwhile, advanced AI-powered weather forecasting systems have been launched to enhance early warnings for extreme weather events nationwide.

title: Nikhat Zareen misses Asian Games and CWG after shock trial defeat
content: Two-time world champion Nikhat Zareen has been surprisingly defeated in national trials, missing selection for the 2026 Commonwealth and Asian Games. Competing in the 51kg category, Zareen lost to Sakshi Chaudhary, marking a significant upset in Indian boxing. This outcome follows recent inconsistencies and a shift to a trial-based selection system.

title: Mamata in lawyer’s robes at HC: Bar Council seeks verification of ex-CM’s practice status
content: The Bar Council of India has requested detailed information from the West Bengal Bar Council regarding Mamata Banerjee's legal enrollment and practice status. This follows her appearance in lawyer's robes at the Calcutta High Court to argue a public interest litigation. The BCI seeks to verify her active status as an advocate, given her previous tenure as Chief Minister.

title: 'Can China, US transcend Thucydides Trap?' Xi's poser to Trump reveals big stakes
content: Chinese President Xi Jinping invoked the "Thucydides Trap" during talks with US President Donald Trump, highlighting concerns that the rising power's growth could lead to conflict with the established dominant power. This theory, popularized by Graham Allison, suggests structural tensions make war more likely, a concept now central to global geopolitical debates involving trade wars and technological competition.

title: ‘Ab toh India ke liye thodi na khelna hai...’: Kohli’s remark goes viral — watch
content: Virat Kohli's scintillating 105* off 60 balls powered Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a six-wicket victory over Kolkata Knight Riders.  The star batter's playful banter with Manish Pandey, caught on stump mic, also captured fan attention.  Kohli's century propelled him past 14,000 T20 runs, making him the fastest Indian to achieve this milestone.

title: ‘Karuppu’ new release date: Suriya and Trisha starrer confirmed for May 15
content: Suriya and Trisha's fantasy action thriller 'Karuppu' faced last-minute show cancellations today, May 14, due to reported financial issues. PVR Cinemas indicated a new release date of May 15, 2026, though official confirmation from the makers is pending. The film, directed by RJ Balaji, centers on a fight for justice.

title: Chiefs linked to former Dolphins WR amid growing injury concerns
content: The Kansas City Chiefs recorded a pretty sharp decline in the team’s performance in 2024 after a Super Bowl loss in the 2024 season, sparking major concern among fans. With just 6 wins and 11 losses, the Kansas City Chiefs missed the playoffs for the first time since 2014. Ahead of the new season, the team remains focused on its roster.

title: 'Unilateral coercive steps, sanctions': Jaishankar takes veiled dig at US
content: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar criticized "unilateral coercive measures" and sanctions, stating they disproportionately harm developing nations and cannot replace diplomacy. He also voiced concern over the West Asia crisis, emphasizing the need for uninterrupted maritime traffic and a sustained ceasefire in Gaza, advocating for a two-state solution.

title: Trump invites Xi to White House after Beijing summit talks
content: In a significant diplomatic gesture, President Trump has welcomed Chinese President Xi Jinping and his spouse to the White House on September 24th, calling it a historic meeting of minds. Central to their discussions was the crucial issue of ensuring stable global energy flows through the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, with both leaders underlining its significance.

title: Tilak Varma named India A captain; Sooryavanshi, Arya included for SL tri-series
content: Tilak Varma will lead the 15-member India A squad in a one-day tri-series in Sri Lanka, featuring Sri Lanka A and Afghanistan A. The team includes emerging talents like Priyansh Arya and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, with Riyan Parag as vice-captain. The series will be followed by two multi-day matches against Sri Lanka A.

title: 'Everything is made up': Pat Cummins rubbishes claims of SA20 NOC from CA
content: Pat Cummins has vehemently denied reports suggesting senior Australian cricketers might skip the home summer for lucrative overseas franchise leagues. While acknowledging the growing financial pull of T20 tournaments, Cummins stated that claims about SA20 NOCs and The Hundred offers are fabricated. He emphasized that current Australian players prioritize representing their country, though this commitment may not last indefinitely.

title: Zverev denied entry at Rome restaurant before Italian Open defeat
content: Alexander Zverev endured a difficult 24 hours in Rome after being denied entry to a famous restaurant and then crashing out of the Italian Open. The World No. 3 was recently turned away from the popular Japanese restaurant Zuma in central Rome because he did not have a reservation. Reports also claimed that the restaurant staff failed to recognise the German tennis star when he arrived with his brother Mischa Zverev and a friend for dinner.

title: Jannik Sinner and Laila Hasanovic’s combined net worth in 2026
content: Jannik Sinner and Laila Hasanovic’s combined net worth in 2026 is estimated at around $35 million to $40 million. Most of that wealth comes from Sinner’s tennis prize money, ATP success, and endorsement deals with global brands. Hasanovic contributes through modeling, fashion partnerships, and social media promotions. Sinner’s growing success in international tennis continues driving the couple’s financial growth.

title: Watch: UP man swept into air while holding tin shed during violent storm, survives fall
content: A terrifying incident in Bareilly, Uttar Pradesh, saw a youth named Nanhe lifted several feet into the air along with a massive tin shed during a powerful dust storm. Villagers witnessed the shed being uprooted and Nanhe clinging to it before being thrown into a maize field. He is currently in critical condition with multiple fractures and severe injuries.

title: India squads for Afghanistan Test, ODIs likely to be picked on May 19
content: Indian selectors are poised to finalise squads for the Afghanistan Test and subsequent ODIs on May 19. Shubman Gill is expected to lead a strong batting lineup for the Test. With Jasprit Bumrah likely to be rested for the contest that doesn't form part of the World Test Championship, plenty of fresh faces are expected to make the cut. Regarding the ODI selection, no major surprises are expected

title: Gurgaon student held for NEET paper leak is on school banner of top achievers
content: A studious farmer's son, Yash Yadav, hailed as an achiever by his local school, has been arrested by Rajasthan police in connection with the NEET-UG paper leak.  Investigators suspect he received and sold a 'guess paper' for lakhs, potentially implicating him in a wider racket that has disrupted the medical entrance exam for lakhs of aspirants.

title: Tamil Nadu govt employees get 2% DA hike as CM Vijay raises allowance to 60%
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay has announced a 2% dearness allowance hike for state government employees and teachers, raising it from 58% to 60% effective January 1. This decision, benefiting approximately 16 lakh individuals, aligns with the Union government's revision and will incur an additional annual expenditure of ₹1,230 crore for the state exchequer.

title: SC bench slams illegal sand mining in Rajasthan; top officials summoned on May 19
content: The Supreme Court has taken a firm stand against the Rajasthan government, holding them directly accountable for the illegal extraction of sand within the National Chambal Sanctuary, a crucial habitat for endangered wildlife like gharials and dolphins.

title: West Bengal makes ‘Vande Mataram’ must in school morning assemblies
content: West Bengal schools must now sing 'Vande Mataram' daily during morning assembly. This directive follows a Union Home Ministry notice. School heads face confusion regarding the mandatory singing of the state song 'Banglar mati Banglar jal' alongside 'Vande Mataram' and the National Anthem. Some schools have already begun implementing the order, while others await clarification before the summer break concludes.

title: Watch: Lawyer Mamata dons black gown, heckled outside Calcutta HC
content: Former Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee appeared as counsel at the Calcutta High Court, presenting evidence of post-poll violence. She alleged that children and women were not spared and submitted a list of alleged murder victims, urging the court to protect the people of Bengal. The Trinamool Congress lauded her commitment to truth and justice.

title: Why is stock market up? Sensex jumps 1,000 points; Nifty50 above 23,700 - top reasons
content: Despite the bullish undertone in equities, several risk factors continue to keep investors on edge. The rupee touched a fresh record low after breaching the 95.8 mark against the US dollar for the first time.

title: Green card, citizenship application processing continues to face delays
content: New USCIS fingerprint checks are causing significant delays for immigration applications, including permanent residency and citizenship. Officials must resubmit biometrics for pending cases to the FBI's upgraded system, even if previously cleared. Attorneys warn this could lead to increased scrutiny of minor past interactions and disproportionately impact immigrants from certain regions.

title: 'Question paper circulated through couriers': Jaipur's Biwal family at centre of NEET paper leak
content: The CBI has arrested five and detained four in the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak, uncovering a multi-state distribution network originating in Pune. A Rajasthan family, the Biwals, is central to the probe, with investigators reconstructing the leak's path through couriers and digital channels. The investigation was triggered by a whistleblower's alert.

title: ‘They hijacked the body’: RG Kar parents move court, demand arrest of ex-MLA Nirmal Ghosh
content: The mother of a rape-murder victim, now an MLA, has moved court seeking the arrest of three Trinamool Congress leaders, alleging they attempted to destroy evidence and hastily cremated her daughter's body. The court has fixed June 5 for the next hearing, with the CBI stating they will submit a response regarding the arrests.

title: 806 Arrested, 667 Kg Drugs Seized: Crackdown On Narco-Terror Network In J&K
content: One of the biggest actions came in Anantnag, where police attached properties worth Rs 6.17 crore in a single case.

title: Ex Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan To Be Leader Of Opposition In Kerala
content: After being elected as the CPI(M) legislature party leader, Pinarayi Vijayan is likely to be chosen as the Leader of the Opposition in the Kerala Assembly.

title: Simultaneous Elections Panel To Hold Consultations In Bengaluru, Gandhinagar
content: The consultations are aimed at understanding the constitutional, administrative, financial and logistical implications of holding simultaneous elections in India.

title: Saffron Shawls Not Allowed: Siddaramaiah Clarifies Hijab Order
content: Siddaramaiah's clarification follows his government's decision to allow students to wear hijab, sacred thread, turban, and other religious symbols.

title: Kerala CM Announcement LIVE Updates: VD Satheesan To Take Oath On May 18
content: Kerala Chief Minister Live Updates: A formal announcement is expected today, once Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge returns to Delhi and holds further talks with senior figures, including Sonia Gandhi.

title: "Make It Work And Never Mess It Up," Xi Tells Trump At Beijing Summit
content: "The world is changing and turbulent," Xi said and noted that the relationship between the two countries impacts not just the 1.7 billion people who live in both countries but also the 8 billion people who live outside the two countries.

title: UK Health Minister Resigns, Says Lost Confidence In PM Starmer's Leadership
content: Wes Streeting, 43, did not say whether he had the required support of 81 Labour MPs -- 20 per cent of the party in parliament -- to trigger a contest.

title: Akhilesh Yadav Gives Chocolate To Niece At Prateek's Funeral, Video Viral
content: The funeral was held at Baikunth Dham (Bhainsa Kund) and attended by thousands, including members of the extended Yadav family, politicians and supporters.

title: Dominated Escalation Matrix On All 4 Days: Defence Chief On Op Sindoor
content: The CDS asserted that Indian forces had situational awareness not only on their own side but also across the border, and that "we were able to dominate every ladder of escalation".

title: BJP Hints At Repealing Old Land Law To Unlock Growth In Bengal
content: The law targets the concentration of urban land by capping private holdings in designated urban agglomerations like Kolkata, Asansol, and Durgapur.

title: New BJP Government Makes 'Vande Mataram' Must In Bengal Schools
content: Earlier this year, the central government accorded 'Vande Mataram' a status equal to the country's national anthem, 'Jana Gana Mana'.

title: 'Lawyer' Mamata Banerjee Argues Case In Court, Now In Bar Council Crosshairs
content: The Bar Council of India has written to the state Bar Council today, seeking that it "furnish information regarding enrolment and practice status of Mamata Banerjee, former Chief Minister of West Bengal".

title: Assam Teacher Beats Students, Tries To Throw One Out Of Window, Arrested
content: The incident came to light after the school's head teacher reviewed CCTV footage installed inside the classroom.

title: Opinion: Opinion | As Chief Minister, VD Satheesan Will Now Have To Learn To Dance To Many Congress Tunes
content: Satheesan's independent "rebel" streak is precisely what worries the High Command; they are loath to lose control over Thiruvananthapuram the way they have over Bengaluru.

title: "UAE Directly Involved In Act Of Aggression Against Iran": Abbas Araghchi
content: Araghchi also referred to what Israel has described as a "secret" meeting in the UAE during the war between Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and UAE President Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan -- a visit Abu Dhabi has denied took place.

title: "Executive Controlling Everything": Top Court On Poll Body Chief Selection
content: The top court was hearing a matter related to the appointment process of the Chief Election Commissioner and Election Commissioners.

title: A 3-km Journey Sums Up The Big Political Journey Of Kerala's VD Satheesan
content: When the Congress-led UDF was decimated in 2021, few imagined Satheesan would be the man to make that journey.

title: Trump China Visit LIVE Updates: Trump Invites Xi Jinping For Visit At White House In September
content: Donald Trump's 2-Day China Visit Live Update: During the trip, US arms sales to Taiwan, China's controls on rare-earth exports, and the countries' raucous trade relationships are among the main issues expected to be taken up by Trump and Xi.

title: Watch: Congress ends suspense: V. D. Satheesan to be next Kerala CM | Above the Fold | 14.05.2026
content: In today’s episode: Kerala finally gets its next Chief Minister as the Congress names V.D. Satheesan for the top post after weeks of suspense and internal discussions. We also track the BRICS Foreign Ministers’ meeting hosted by India in New Delhi, where External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar addressed key global challenges and diplomatic priorities.

title: BJP questions Rahul Gandhi’s foreign trips; Congress calls it a diversion
content: BJP demands Rahul disclose the source of the funds for the trips; Cong. demands answers on economy, jobs instead

title: HDK urges government to drop Bidadi township project
content: Incidentally, the Bidadi township was first announced during the 20-month regime of Mr. Kumaraswamy during his first term as the Chief Minister in 2006-2007.

title: SIR is weakening democracy, say activists
content: In 150 Assembly seats in West Bengal, the winning margin was less than voter deletions due to SIR, says Anjali Bharadwaj, questioning the fairness of the polls; vote dacoity took place openly, alleges Harsh Mander

title: BJP’s three seats will change Kerala’s history: Kummanam Rajasekharan
content: Drawing a parallel with other States, Kummanam says that the BJP rose to power in West Bengal and Tripura after starting with just three seats and a similar trajectory is now inevitable for Kerala

title: About 25 foreign nationals arrested for fraudulent voting in Tamil Nadu Assembly poll
content: The accused persons, carrying foreign passports, were intercepted by officials of the Bureau of Immigration and detained by the police after indelible ink was found on their index fingers

title: Operation to capture and relocate Indian gaurs in Chikkamagaluru: No progress on the first day due to rains
content: The operation is said to be the first-of-its-kind in south India. The officers have taken it as an experiment to develop an SOP to capture Indian gaurs

title: Online OP ticket booking system fully operational in govt. hospitals in Wayanad
content: The application allows users to identify hospitals with speciality doctors and book appointments easily. Information regarding laboratory tests and their rates, blood banks, pay wards, radiology services, and ambulance facilities can also be accessed instantly

title: Global Travel Market selects Andhra Pradesh as ‘Best Tourism Promotion State’
content: It invites Tourism Minister Kandula Durgesh to receive the award at a function to be organised on June 4 in Kovalam

title: One dead, several injured in landslide in Jamuria coal mines in West Bengal; many feared missing
content: It is reported that, during the blasting process of mining, there was “air blast” in section 27 of the Kunustoria coal mines, leading to collapse of multiple pillars and trigging a landslide. Workers alleged lack of supervision and safety measures

title: CREDAI property expo: prizes distributed
content: A total of 18 winners were awarded gold coins. From among them, two winners were selected through a lucky draw to receive bumper prizes of ₹3 lakh and ₹2 lakh respectively

title: Cabinet nod for 25 major projects with investments worth ₹2 lakh crore
content: Projects by Reliance Industries, Apollo Tyres, Royal Enfield and Carrier Airconditioning are among those cleared; a defence R&D facility in Amaravati and ‘Sanjeevani’ digital healthcare system also get the green signal

title: Protest in Hubballi against allowing hijab in educational institutions
content: Members of Hindutva outfits led by Sri Rama Sene chief Pramod Mutalik condemn the decision to lift the ban on wearing hijab in schools, colleges

title: Woman gang-raped inside bus in Delhi’s Rani Bagh, two arrested
content: The two accused have been arrested in connection with the incident that took place on May 11, the sources said

title: CPI(M) picks Pinarayi Vijayan as Leader of Opposition in Kerala Assembly
content: The development sets the stage once again for the high-voltage political rivalry between V.D. Satheesan and Pinarayi Vijayan that dominated the Assembly over the last five years.

title: 89 dead as heavy rain, lightning wreak havoc across Uttar Pradesh
content: Damage reported in 19 districts; 53 people injured, 87 houses collapsed or damaged; U.P. CM directs officials to provide updates every three hours, aid victims, assess damage and release compensation

title: Officials told to remain alert to tackle drinking water issues during summer
content: Minister says a ₹7,000-crore project has been proposed to supply water from Narayanpur Reservoir to 370 villages in Bidar, Kalaburagi districts

title: V.D. Satheesan calls on A.K. Antony, V.M. Sudheeran
content: A.K. Antony urges V.D. Satheesan be granted time to address Kerala’s deepening financial crisis. He emphasises that Satheesan faces a daunting challenge, recalling his own experience in 2001 when initial public support quickly waned amid tough governmental decisions.

title: Chittoor officials urged to make plans for 3,106 tanks to boost water conservation
content: The Collector instructs officials to inspect all minor irrigation tanks and verify restoration of feeder canals while reviewing “Jaladhara-Neeti Bhadrata” initiatives in Palamaner constituency

title: A.P. govt. plans public awareness campaign on austerity measures
content: CM Naidu calls for responsible spending measures such as every officer must use one official vehicle, wider use of virtual meetings and feasibility study on work-from-home system in suitable sectors

title: Bulldozer razes Trinamool party office, clock tower in West Bengal
content: Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari has said the government will have a “zero-tolerance policy” towards illegal structures; while the tower was declared illegal by a court, the office was built on Railway land

title: Congress to join TVK-led govt. in T.N.; number of Cabinet berths to be finalised: Girish Chodankar
content: We have already received the offer from TVK. We have accepted the offer to be part of the government after nearly 60 years, says AICC T.N. in-charge

title: Suchitwa Mission launches campaign promoting reuse culture among schoolchildren
content: The campaign aims to cultivate the habit of reuse among students, reduce the excessive consumption of natural resources, and ensure waste reduction

title: PM’s appeal was for intelligent substitution of consumption of import-heavy goods: PMEAC member
content: If the country can reduce forex outflows by 10% in a year, we will be saving close to ₹2.5 lakh crore of foreign exchange outflow per year, says Gourav Vallabh, member of the Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister; he adds that the Prime Minister’s call for a considered change in consumption pattern was an appeal above all else

title: Siddaramaiah reviews two major infra projects worth ₹564 crore in Mysuru
content: The ambitious road modernisation project aims to improve urban mobility, ease traffic congestion, strengthen tourism infrastructure and provide durable pothole-free roads amid Mysuru’s rapid urban growth and increasing tourist inflow

title: Traffic congestion on Kannur-Kozhikode NH worsens amid delay in widening work
content: As per the first construction agreement, the road widening work on the Vengalam-Azhiyur stretch was expected to be completed by April 2024. Even a year after the deadline, the stretch, which was split into four segments for speedy execution, remains incomplete

title: Collector urges officials to improve academic standards in Annamayya district
content: The district is currently ranked sixth in the State under the “Badi Pilustondi” enrolment programme, says Nishant Kumar reviewing the performance of schools and Intermediate education, Samagra Siksha Abhiyan and Skill Development wings

title: Tamil Nadu Minister Keerthana apologises for remarks on persons with disability
content: “I too, am a daughter of a disabled mother. I have that pain too. I appeal not to politicise my words made out of ignorance,” wrote the TVK Minister in her social media post.

title: CPI leaders call for prompt support to Chittoor mango farmers
content: Party’s State executive member urges Chief Minister to order the timely processing of works in mango pulp factories and ensure remunerative prices for mango produce

title: 37 workers from Odisha rescued in Vijayanagar district
content: Acting on information provided by a Belagavi-based NGO, officials raid a worksite at Nandibandi village and safely send the workers back home

title: K. Balakrishnan, veteran editor of Express, passes away at 87
content: A prominent figure in Kerala’s media landscape, Balakrishnan played a key role in shaping the identity and influence of Express, a former Thrissur-based Malayalam daily, over several decades

title: EC announces Phase III of SIR: 16 states 3 UTs to be covered - check list

content: Sreejesh Parattu Raveendran of looks on during the Men's Quarter Final match between India and Great Britain on day nine of the Olympic Games Paris 2024 at Stade Yves Du Manoir on August 04, 2024 in Paris, France.
